{{Quote|Wasaki now stored in hold, waiting delivery and my 100,000 creds. Must be boring back there. Maybe I should let him out—he's harmless without blades.|Leshy Drobo's personal log entry|<ref name="Disturbance" />}}
The '''''Starkiller''''' was a [[Frommon longhaul all-purpose transport|freighter]] used by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Leshy Drobo]] prior to [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]]. Drobo and [[Gender/Legends|his]] partner, the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[XT-8]], captured the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Roni von Wasaki]], on whom a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] had been placed. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] was [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|carbon-frozen]] and [[Transport/Legends|transported]] in a secret compartment aboard the ''Starkiller'' to the [[Unidentified crime boss|crime boss]] who had originated the bounty. While the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was still in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] transit, however, Wasaki, while still [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]], planted a [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] [[Mind trick/Legends|suggestion]] in Drobo's [[Brain/Legends|mind]] so that the latter would release the former. When XT-8 learned of his partner's intention, the [[XT labor droid|droid]] attacked and [[Death/Legends|killed]] him but incidentally also damaged the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. The ship reverted to [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]], and XT-8 simply resigned to carrying on the trip at [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speed, which made the journey drag on for twenty [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]].

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], another starship emerged out of hyperspace due to the presence of the ''Starkiller'', which by that [[Time/Legends|time]] was rumored to be a [[ghost ship]]. One of the ship's crew-members informed the others about the ancient history of the ''Starkiller'', and the group eventually boarded the vessel. Aboard the ship, the explorers faced XT-8, who attempted to eliminate the boarders, and eventually learned about the ship's fate and discovered and liberated Wasaki, who had gone mad due to the thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] spent imprisoned in carbonite. The group of individuals and Wasaki subsequently departed from the ''Starkiller'' aboard the other starship.

==Description==
===Design and transportation capabilities===
The ''Starkiller'' was a [[Frommon longhaul all-purpose transport]], one of the earliest [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump-capable [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] models of the early period of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The thirty-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]], designed for a crew of two, had a shape that narrowed toward its bow, and two [[Engine/Legends|engine]] pods, containing a single [[Ion coil engine|ion coil]] [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight engine]] each, were located at the ship's stern. The name of the starship was stenciled across the side of the vessel in [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Writing/Legends|lettering]], and the ''Starkiller'' was equipped with a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]], a [[Navigation computer/Legends|nav computer]], and a [[Life support/Legends|life support]] system that provided the ship with a breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

The ''Starkiller''{{'s}} cargo hold, with a capacity of one hundred [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]], was situated between the two engine pods. A lift at the aft section of the hold could be dropped in order to load and unload cargo, but such an action was prevented by a [[security lock]] while the ship was in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Inside the cargo hold, four [[grav-net]]s allowed for the securing of crates. A hidden compartment was also installed under the hold's floor. The engine room containing the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] access panel was located amidships, forward of the cargo hold. The ''Starkiller'' was equipped with a Class 4 hyperdrive with no [[Backup hyperdrive|backup drive]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

===The bridge and the lounge===
[[File:StarkillerHologram-Challenge66.png|thumb|right|200px|The hologram displayed by the holoprojector in the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} lounge]]
The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of the ''Starkiller'' was located at the foremost section of the ship's bow. It had a [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] as well as two chairs in front of the bridge control console. The firing [[Computer/Legends|computer]] of the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] [[Laser turret/Legends|turret]] could be linked to the console. The ship's computer banks could also be accessed from the latter, although unsuccessful attempts at [[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] into the computer system triggered a trap: a [[Blast door/Legends|blast door]] sealed the bridge off from the rest of the ship and ventilation ducts began sucking [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] out of the room. In the bridge, the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]] could be engaged, and unsuccessful tampering with a [[computer terminal]] elsewhere aboard the ship, as well as indiscreet electronic bypassing of the security lock of the vessel's inner [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] hatch, sounded an [[Alarm system/Legends|alarm]] in the bridge.<ref name="Disturbance" />

The ''Starkiller'', although having no capacity for passengers, had a lounge that was situated abaft of the bridge. The room was furnished with a table and two [[com-form chair]]s. It also had a viewport, red emergency lighting, and a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojection disc]] that was located in the center of the room. The controls for the holoprojector were installed in the lounge's furnishing, and when activated, the device displayed a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of a black-colored creature with membranous [[Limb/Legends|wings]] and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] filled with [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]] that were dripping [[Poison/Legends|venom]]. The hologram at first emitted a loud, screeching sound and then ascended from the floor, before suddenly spreading its wings and looming over whomever was watching the display.<ref name="Disturbance" />

===Other rooms===
Situated in the outer corner of the lounge that adjoined the bridge, the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} [[Refresher/Legends|head]] was equipped with washing and crude waste-disposal facilities. It also had a [[Toilet/Legends|toilet]], opposite of which was a [[Viewscreen/Legends|vidscreen]] and a [[Medpac/Legends|medpack]] hanged next to it. The vidscreen was [[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] with periodic-files for reading purposes. The starship's small airlock was located at the other outer corner of the lounge, and it consisted of an outer hatch, an inner hatch, and the airlock proper, which was bare save for a security lock next to the inner hatch. The interior aft corner of the lounge contained what appeared to be an ordinary storage locker. An emergency [[Environment suit/Legends|space suit]] was hanged in the back of the room, and on the floor there were two boxes containing various junk. A hidden hatch in the room's ceiling, however, concealed a retractable ladder leading up to the firing seat of the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} [[Laser/Legends|laser]] [[Turret/Legends|turret]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

Also adjacent to the lounge was the galley of the ''Starkiller'', which was stocked with everything related to the storage and preparation of [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], including an [[autochef]]. The starship held two [[Standard month/Legends|months']] worth of consumables. Situated abaft of the galley, the [[Captain/Legends|captain's]] quarters included a [[Sleeper (furniture)|bed]], a chair, and a desk. A computer terminal which allowed access to the ship's computer system was mounted on the top of the desk. Set into the aft wall of the room, a storage cubicle housed a [[Armor/Legends|suit]] of [[bounty hunter armor]], a [[Force pike/Legends|force pike]], and a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

===Changes over time===
At some point, the upper [[Hull/Legends|hull]] of the ''Starkiller'' was modified, and the starship was publicly listed as having an armament rating of zero, which meant that it carried no weapons at all, despite the laser cannon the ship was outfitted with in reality. During the starship's twenty [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]–long journey at sublight speed, it became heavily outmoded, with its ion coil engines being crude by the standards of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

During its journey, the ''Starkiller'' also became battered, with its name being barely legible by the [[Time/Legends|time]] the vessel was encountered during the Galactic Civil War. At some point, a collision with a [[Meteorite/Legends|meteor]] dented and jammed shut the outer hatch of the ship's airlock. The air aboard the vessel had become stale, and a thick layer of dust covered everything in the lounge, to the extent that the holoprojector in the room's center was hidden by it. The emergency lighting in the lounge had also turned on, and the autochef in the ship's galley malfunctioned.<ref name="Disturbance" />

==History==
===A Jedi captured===
{{Quote|While Wasaki concentrated on me, XT-8 snuck behind and stunned him.|Leshy Drobo's personal log entry|<ref name="Disturbance" />}}
[[File:SamuraiDelicatessenInSpace-Challenge66.png|thumb|left|220px|The carbon-frozen Jedi Knight Roni von Wasaki was transported aboard the ''Starkiller''.]]
The ''Starkiller'' was owned by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Leshy Drobo]], who lived prior to<ref name="Disturbance" /> [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]]. At some point by that date,<ref name="Date">{{ChallengeCite|66|Disturbance in the Force (article)}} establishes that [[Leshy Drobo]] captured [[Roni von Wasaki]] more than twenty thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which corresponds to some point prior to [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Drobo and [[Gender/Legends|his]] partner, the [[XT labor droid]]-turned-[[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[XT-8]], captured the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Roni von Wasaki]], for whom a [[Unidentified crime boss|crime boss]] had posted a one hundred thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]]-large [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]]. The pair of bounty [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] subsequently [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|froze]] the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in a slab of [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] to deliver him to the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]]. Drobo confiscated Wasaki's pair of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and stashed them in the storage cubicle of the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} captain's quarters. Subsequently, the ship, with the frozen Jedi hidden in its secret compartment and its cargo hold containing fifty crates of what was marked and listed in the register as [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] parts but was in reality useless junk, was on its way to deliver the bounty.<ref name="Disturbance" />

Following Wasaki's capture, Drobo also noted that fact in an entry of his personal log that was stored in the computer system of the ''Starkiller''. By that point, Wasaki, who had retained his [[Brain/Legends|mental]] faculties and [[Force-sensitive/Legends|use]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] while frozen, had begun to [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] [[Mind trick/Legends|influence]] Drobo to release him from the carbonite. Shortly after the ''Starkiller'' had jumped to hyperspace, Wasaki finally implanted a similar suggestion in Drobo's mind that the bounty hunter began to act upon. However, XT-8 learned of its partner's intentions and confronted Drobo in the ship's engine room. During the ensuing confrontation, the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] opened fire at Drobo from its built-in [[submachinegun]]. As a result, Drobo was [[Death/Legends|killed]] after being riddled with [[Slug/Legends|bullets]], but the nearby hyperdrive access panel had also been perforated by XT-8's attack. The damage to the ship's superluminal drive led to the vessel reverting to realspace.<ref name="Disturbance" />

===The long journey===
Since repairing the ''Starkiller'' was beyond XT-8's programming, the droid simply continued its journey under sublight speed, turning a trip of what could have been mere thirty [[Standard day/Legends|days]] into a journey that lasted for twenty thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Over time, the assassin droid's mission—to deliver the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} cargo to its destination at any cost—became burned into its memory circuits, and by the Galactic Civil War, most of the ship's power had been rerouted into its sublight engines.<ref name="Disturbance" />

Meanwhile, Wasaki, who was being kept alive by his carbonite [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisonment]], continued reaching out via the Force in order to contact anyone who could liberate him. The perpetual loneliness, fearful [[Dream/Legends|dreams]], and extreme sensory deprivation that the Jedi endured eventually drove him mad. Every so often over the subsequent millennia, failed Jedi and [[Alcohol/Legends|drunken]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] would claim that they had encountered the ''Starkiller'', and eventually a rumor began to spread that it was a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] vessel. According to the rumor, the ''Starkiller'' had depressurized during a raid, leading to the death of its crew, which nevertheless somehow remained [[Ghost ship|connected]] to the material realm. However, at the same time, knowledge of the name and the model of the ship, as well as of the name and the actual occupation of the ship's real owner, was also preserved by history.<ref name="Disturbance" />

{{RPG|"[[Disturbance in the Force (article)|Disturbance in the Force]]"}}
===An encounter in deep space===
{{Quote|Not alone…found living souls. Can't escape…can't…leave me alone…|Roni von Wasaki, making telepathic contact with one of the crew-members of the starship that encountered the ''Starkiller'' during the Galactic Civil War|<ref name="Disturbance" />}}
At some point during the Galactic Civil War, another starship traveling through hyperspace, while still far from its destination, unexpectedly emerged in realspace close to the ''Starkiller''. The latter ship, while not making any attempts at communication, was on a trajectory that intersected the flight path of the newly-arrived vessel, and the [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogator]] of the ship pointed the ''Starkiller'' out to the rest of the crew through the viewport of the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

A crew-member familiar with ancient criminal history then identified the vessel and revealed a portion of its history to the rest of the crew, and one of the spacers also noticed the ship's modified upper hull. The [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] of the group's starship did not detect any life-forms aboard the ''Starkiller'' and informed that the vessel's power had been rerouted to its engines. Attempts by a Force-sensitive member of the group to discern the presence of any living beings aboard the unusual ship were similarly unsuccessful, although at that point Wasaki finally established telepathic contact, albeit vague and almost incoherent one, with one of the crew-members.<ref name="Disturbance" />

===Exploring the ''Starkiller''===
[[File:XT8-Challenge66.png|thumb|right|260px|XT-8 attacked the group that boarded the ''Starkiller''.]]
When the starship finally approached the ''Starkiller'' for boarding, the latter [[Space warfare/Legends|opened fire]] and attempted to escape the former. After the chase led to the ''Starkiller'' suffering severe damage, its engines shut down, and the ancient vessel appeared to be dead in space. At that point, XT-8, who sat at the control console in the bridge at the time, began repeatedly striking the viewport in an attempt to shatter it and therefore [[Explosive decompression|expose]] the would-be boarders to the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of space. The [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the other starship then pulled it alongside the ''Starkiller'' and attached the [[landing claw]], after which the crew entered the drifting freighter through its airlock.<ref name="Disturbance" />

Once aboard the ''Starkiller'', the explorers accidentally activated the hologram in the ship's lounge, with one of the startled individuals firing a weapon at the insubstantial image and thereby damaging the room. The boarding party also discovered the concealed ladder that allowed access to the starship's laser turret, and upon entering the bridge were immediately attacked by XT-8, who then attempted to force the battle out into the lounge, thereby lessening the risk of accidentally damaging the functionality of the vessel. The group eventually defeated the droid and accessed via the bridge control console both Drobo's personal log entry and the publicly-available shipping manifest of the ''Starkiller'' from the ship's computer system, albeit triggering the built-in trap in the process, which it eventually escaped.<ref name="Disturbance" />

During their exploration of the ''Starkiller'', the group repaired the vessel's damaged hyperdrive and eventually discovered the secret compartment in the cargo hold where the frozen body of Wasaki was located. After de-carbonizing the twenty-thousand-year-old Jedi, both the explorers and the ancient Jedi left the ''Starkiller'' and departed aboard the starship the group had arrived on.<ref name="Disturbance" />
{{Endgame}}

==Owners and operators==
{{Quote|Leshy Drobo was actually a bounty hunter and his freight-hauling a cover for illegal operations.|One of the crewmembers of the starship that encountered the ''Starkiller''|<ref name="Disturbance" />}}
The ''Starkiller'' was privately owned by the bounty hunter Leshy Drobo. In order to conceal his illegal activities, Drobo maintained a cover identity of a [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] who ran a freight-hauling business that eventually made him wealthy. Drobo partnered with the droid XT-8 and also with an individual named [[Gammid]], but the latter was considered to be incompetent by Drobo and eventually died during the trio's capture of the Jedi Knight Roni von Wasaki. After XT-8 killed Drobo during a confrontation over the latter intending to release the captured Wasaki from his carbonite [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]], the assassin droid became the sole surviving crew-member of the ''Starkiller''. The droid spent time in the starship's bridge, controlling its flight and weapons systems as well as monitoring the built-in alarm system.<ref name="Disturbance" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Starships-DisturbanceInTheForce.png|thumb|right|250px|An illustration in "Disturbance in the Force" depicted two starships without specifying whether any of them was the ''Starkiller''.]]
The ''Starkiller'' served as the main setting for "[[Disturbance in the Force (article)|Disturbance in the Force]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] mini-adventure written by [[Chris Hind]] as a supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', illustrated by [[Paul Daly]], and published in [[Challenge 66|''Challenge'' 66]] in [[November]] [[1992]]. Beside the starship's deckplan, "Disturbance in the Force" included another illustration that depicted two different vessels, but the adventure did not specify which one, if any, of them corresponded to the ''Starkiller''.<ref name="Disturbance" />

"Disturbance in the Force" suggests the airlock of the ''Starkiller'' as the most likely point of entry the player characters can use to board the vessel, but it also notes that the characters can, using either explosives or a lightsaber or a [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusion cutter]] for slicing, create an entrance at any other point of the ship's outer hull. Damaging the engine pods of the ship in such a manner, however, is characterized as potentially "messy." Additionally, if the player characters do not succeed in finding in the cargo hold the secret compartment containing the frozen Jedi, Roni von Wasaki, the adventure suggests that one of the characters may receive a telepathic suggestion from the prisoner that helps with the search.<ref name="Disturbance" />

During the events of "Disturbance in the Force," the player characters disable the ''Starkiller''. Furthermore, the adventure also notes that "if" that has occurred, XT-8 starts trying to shatter the bridge viewport in order to expose the approaching boarders to the vacuum of space. However, an apparent contradiction exists due to the adventure also stating that XT-8 abandons the bridge in search of the characters if they trigger an alarm through slicing into a computer terminal or the security lock of a hatch. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Disturbance" />

==Appearances==
